items.passage: The hungry boy ___ his lunch and ___ a glass of water.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: You 'ate' the lunch and 'drank' the water.

items.passage: Wei Ming ___ the ball and his friend ___ it back.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: You 'kicked' the ball and your friend 'passed' it back.

items.passage: The weather was very ___, so we stayed in the ___ of a big tree.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: It was 'hot', so we stood in the 'shade' to keep cool.

items.passage: Siti opened her ___ and began to ___ the first chapter.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: You open a 'book' and then 'read' a chapter.

items.passage: The bird flew high into the ___ and sang a sweet ___.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: A bird flies into the 'sky' and sings a 'song'.

items.passage: Kavitha felt ___ after the long run, so she sat down to ___.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: She was 'tired' so she sat to 'rest'.

items.passage: The gardener used a ___ to dig a hole and planted a young ___.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: You dig with a 'spade' and plant a 'tree'.

items.passage: Ravi washed his ___ with soap and dried them on a clean ___.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: You wash your 'hands' and dry them with a 'towel'.

items.passage: The night sky was ___, and thousands of stars began to ___.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: The sky was 'dark' so the stars could 'shine'.

items.passage: Nurul was ___ when she lost her toy, but she ___ when it was found.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: She was 'sad' to lose it and 'smiled' when it was found.

items.passage: The chef ___ the vegetables and then ___ them in a hot pan.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: You 'chopped' the vegetables and then 'fried' them.

items.passage: Ahmad put on his ___ and rode his ___ to school.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: You wear a 'helmet' and ride a 'bicycle'.

items.passage: The room was so ___ that Meiling had to ___ to be heard.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: In a 'noisy' room you must 'shout' to be heard.

items.passage: The rain was ___, so the boys ran to find ___.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: In 'heavy' rain you look for 'shelter'.

items.passage: Grandma threaded the ___ and began to ___ a button onto the shirt.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: You thread a 'needle' to 'sew' a button.

items.passage: The sun was ___ and the ice cream began to ___.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: In the 'hot' sun, ice cream will 'melt'.

items.passage: The old man was very ___, so a boy offered him a ___ on the bus.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: Because he was 'weak', the boy offered him a 'seat'.

items.passage: Siti ___ the seeds every day, and soon green shoots began to ___.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.
items.rationale: She 'watered' the seeds so the shoots could 'grow'.

items.passage: The team ___ hard all season and finally ___ the cup.

items.passage: The path was ___ and slippery, so the hikers walked with great ___.
items.stem: Put the right word in each blank. Each word is used once.

CL-vocab-cloze.iCanStatement: I can choose the right word to fill each blank in a passage.
CL-vocab-cloze.explanation: Read the whole sentence first to see what makes sense. Each blank needs a word that fits the meaning — a naming word, an action word or a describing word. Every word in the bank is used once, so start with the blanks you are most sure of.

workedExamples.text: The hungry boy ate his lunch.
workedExamples.note: 'ate' is the action that fits a hungry boy and lunch.

workedExamples.text: It was hot, so the ice cream melted.
workedExamples.note: 'melted' is what happens to ice cream in the heat.
